packs.title: Paid async workflow
gates: Show payment-confirmed and in-progress states separately.
Expose ETA when known, or honest indeterminate progress when not known.
Provide email, account, or recovery path after browser close.
Show clear DONE, FAILED, retry, and still-working states.
Keep payment/report state customer-readable and accessible.
verification: Exercise success, retryable failure, permanent failure, and browser-close recovery.
Confirm no paid state can silently disappear without customer-visible recovery.

packs.title: Report or output product
gates: Findings are actionable and written in customer-facing language.
Each material claim has proof, source citation, or clear limitation.
Every finding includes remedy or next-step guidance.
No raw enum labels, internal jargon, implementation terms, undefined, null, NaN, or empty list joins appear in customer output.
verification: Run a fixture with raw enum/internal jargon and confirm signoff fails.
Run a fixture with customer copy, citations, and remedies and confirm signoff passes.

packs.title: Compliance or risk product
gates: Classify each claim as fact, signal, automated finding, draft, recommendation, or needs-review.
Fail hard when required external data is unavailable for a regulated claim.
State limitations and confidence plainly.
Do not make unsupported accusations or violation claims.
verification: Confirm unsupported violation wording is rejected or downgraded.

packs.title: Estonian SaaS context
gates: Explain relevance to small Estonian businesses, e-residents, or micro-OUs when applicable.
Use template variables for company, product, route, stack, repo, and project-specific values.
Check Estonian regulatory fit where the workflow touches tax, accounting, privacy, payments, employment, or corporate records.
verification: Scan the generated brief for hardcoded company names, repo paths, or tenant/customer identifiers.
Confirm Estonian-specific claims cite a source or are marked as assumptions.

packs.title: Paid conversion
gates: Make free versus paid value explicit.
Paid output includes free output plus additional customer value.
Options map to outcomes, not raw data sources or implementation knobs.
Payment CTA is near required fields and validation on desktop and mobile.
verification: Check free and paid paths side by side.
Confirm the paid promise is visible before payment and fulfilled after payment.
